name: veil
title: veil

commands.name: veil
commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# veil -h
==========================================================================
                Veil (Setup Script) | [Updated]: 2018-05-08
==========================================================================
    [Web]: https://www.veil-framework.com/ | [Twitter]: @VeilFramework
==========================================================================
 
                os = kali
         osversion = 2026.2
      osmajversion = 2026
              arch = x86_64
          trueuser = kali
  userprimarygroup = kali
       userhomedir = /home/kali
           rootdir = /usr/share/veil
           veildir = /var/lib/veil
         outputdir = /var/lib/veil/output
   dependenciesdir = /var/lib/veil/setup-dependencies
           winedir = /var/lib/veil/wine
         winedrive = /var/lib/veil/wine/drive_c
           gempath = Z:\var\lib\veil\wine\drive_c\Ruby187\bin\gem
 
[I] Kali Linux 2026.2 x86_64 detected...
 
 
 
[?] Are you sure you wish to install Veil?
 
    Continue with installation? ([y]es/[s]ilent/[N]o):

binary_metadata.file_type: Bourne-Again shell script, ASCII text executable

apt_metadata.depends: python3:any, sudo, unzip, git, mingw-w64, mono-mcs, ruby, python3, python3-pycryptodome, wine, metasploit-framework
apt_metadata.description:
Generates payloads to bypass anti-virus solutions
Veil is a tool designed to generate metasploit payloads that bypass
common anti-virus solutions. It replaces the package veil-evasion.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use veil because ### veil.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair veil with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `veil --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### veil.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install veil
